noveltyJustification: Confirmed the zero-caller gap and added implementation blockers: narrow call-site assumption, missing registry edge, canonical-id/provider-slug mapping, approval policy, and fallback-label attribution.
keyQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback?
answeredQuestions: Fallback-router wiring should remain optional/deferred for the structural merge unless the operator explicitly expands scope to behavior changes.

keyFindings.summary: fallback-router.ts is a shipped pure decision primitive with test-only callers and no production dispatch integration.
fanout-pool retries the same item/index/label until maxRetries and then sets retry_exhausted; no model-swap path exists.
Child 004's proposed fanout-pool-only call site is too narrow because fanout-run owns model-bearing lineages, prompt construction, command dispatch, and artifact validation.
The current model profile registry lists both GLM-5.2 and MiMo-V2.5-Pro with fallback_target null, so GLM-to-MiMo fallback would be new policy/configuration.
Fallback router ids differ from executable provider slugs, so integration needs canonical-id to CLI-slug normalization.
The fallback router's approved-model guard means substitution must be explicitly approved by config/policy, not silently inferred from a global edge.
A fallback lineage needs a distinct label/artifact directory and status event to avoid mixing failed GLM retry state with MiMo replacement output.

ruledOut.approach: Make child 004 mandatory inside the structural merge without an operator scope-expansion decision
ruledOut.reason: Fallback wiring is behavior expansion requiring registry/schema, caller approval, model-id normalization, and attribution changes; it is not required for the rename/nesting/reference migration.

recommendedNextFocus: Run synthesis-readiness/convergence pass and produce final research synthesis if no contradictions appear.
